title: Cirrus: Fix decrypt failure
body:
I Noted that the last cron-based branch-build had failed with an error from Cirrus-CI regarding decryption of variables.  Upon examination, found several encrypted values to be invalid.  Replaced the critical ones, and disabled any useless/disabled values/facilities (i.e. IRC notifications, and GCS release uploads).

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: I have no way of reviewing this. I have confirmed that the `a28` string appears in the `master` copy of `.cirrus.yml`, in a context that suggests that it is equivalent usage to the one in this branch. None of the other strings (GCP) appear in master. I can only assume that if CI passes, the strings must be valid.

> I can only assume that if CI passes, the strings must be valid.

This is the case.  I found in my investigation, if ***ANY*** of the strings fail to decrypt, Cirrus-CI will not dercrypt anything and just  try to pass the `ENCRYPTED[blah1234]`...causing things to break very badly.

Thanks for taking a look @edsantiago
